metadata.title: The two-sided chip-chokepoint war — Western equipment (ASML/Zeiss/Japan) vs China's materials leverage
metadata.status: facts from major press/government/company; the kill-switch is reported (graded); the 'mutual chokepoint deterrence' framing is labeled interpretation
metadata.frame: Upstream of Taiwan (geopolitics-taiwan-silicon-shield) the semiconductor chain is a TWO-SIDED chokepoint war: the West controls the EQUIPMENT to make advanced chips (ASML's EUV monopoly + Zeiss optics + Japanese tools/materials) and denies it to China; China controls the raw MATERIALS (gallium, germanium, antimony, graphite, heavy rare earths) and retaliates with extraterritorial export controls. Each side holds a chokepoint over the other - mutual deterrence plus a scramble for substitutes. Completes the chokepoint stack: minerals -> materials -> equipment -> fab -> packaging -> HBM. Overlay; excluded from the proofs. Connects to geopolitics-taiwan-silicon-shield, geopolitics-allied-intel-nodes, macro-critical-minerals-equities, defense_chokepoint.

western_equipment_chokepoint.asml_euv_monopoly: ASML (Netherlands) is the SOLE maker of EUV lithography - ~90% of the lithography market and 100% of EUV, with no competitor in sight. EUV is required for any chip below ~7nm; everyone building advanced chips must come to ASML. ~80% of ASML's EUV sales go to TSMC. status: fact.
western_equipment_chokepoint.zeiss_optics: ASML's EUV optics come from a single partner - Zeiss SMT (Germany); ~80% of all microchips worldwide are made with Zeiss optics + ASML systems. High-NA EUV enters series production in 2026 (an even narrower chokepoint). A monopoly resting on another monopoly. status: fact.
western_equipment_chokepoint.export_controls: Since 2019 the US + Netherlands have barred ASML's advanced EUV (and, increasingly, immersion DUV) from China without a Dutch license - described as 'the single most consequential decision in protecting Western AI dominance.' New US/Dutch curbs in 2026 tightened further. status: fact.
western_equipment_chokepoint.kill_switch: ASML reportedly built a REMOTE KILL SWITCH into TSMC's EUV machines - able to disable them via a maintenance software update if China invades Taiwan - and reportedly reassured the Dutch government it could do so. status: contested (reported, not officially confirmed). Implication: even seizing Taiwan would not hand China working leading-edge EUV - but it would also brick the world's leading edge.
western_equipment_chokepoint.japan_tools_materials: Japan adds a parallel chokepoint: deposition/etch/clean tools (Tokyo Electron) and >50% of 14 critical chip MATERIALS (~75% of high-end photoresist via Shin-Etsu/Tokyo Ohka/JSR/Fujifilm). So 'Western' equipment is really NL (litho) + DE (optics) + JP (tools/materials) + US (Applied/Lam/EDA). status: fact.

china_materials_counter_leverage.the_bans: China's retaliation runs on the materials it dominates: 3 Dec 2024 it banned gallium, germanium, ANTIMONY and superhard materials to the US (direct retaliation for chip controls); 4 Apr 2025 added seven medium/heavy rare earths (Tb, Dy, Sm, Gd, Lu, Sc, Y) - the magnets for motors/defense. status: fact.
china_materials_counter_leverage.extraterritorial_rule: 9 Oct 2025: China's most sweeping controls yet, modeled on the US Foreign Direct Product Rule - ANY foreign-made product containing >=0.1% Chinese-origin rare earths, or made using Chinese processing technology, needs a Chinese license. Beijing extended its regulatory reach across the entire global supply chain (the mirror of US extraterritorial controls). status: fact.
china_materials_counter_leverage.the_truce: Nov 2025: China suspended the US-focused controls (MOFCOM No.46/2024 Art.2) until 27 Nov 2026 as part of a trade truce - but the ARCHITECTURE remains and is reinstatable; tightening is expected late 2026 if conditions deteriorate. status: fact. (Same suspend-but-keep-the-gun pattern as the antimony ban in macro-critical-minerals-equities.)
china_materials_counter_leverage.why_it_bites: China processes ~90% of rare earths, ~90% of antimony, ~most gallium/germanium/graphite - the inputs the West's own fabs and weapons need. The US supply response (MP/Perpetua/UAMY/Ucore) targets exactly these, but on a 2026-2029 timeline. status: fact.

breaking_the_chokepoint_and_the_unknowns.china_euv_multitrack: China is racing on multiple tracks (graded - reported/projected, not delivered): LDP-EUV (Huawei + SMEE + Harbin Inst.) - a Laser-Induced Discharge Plasma source reportedly at 100-150W mid-2025 (vs ASML 250W+ for HVM), enough for 'first-light' 5nm-class testing; a Shenzhen 'whole-of-nation' prototype with SiCarrier in testing; and SSMB-EUV (Tsinghua) - a steady-state-micro-bunching particle-accelerator path that could leapfrog if it works (years out). DUV multipatterning already yields SMIC 7nm (Mate 60) without EUV at a cost/yield penalty. Aggressive 'parity by end of decade' claims are CONTESTED - ambition, not delivery. status: contested.
breaking_the_chokepoint_and_the_unknowns.alternatives: Canon Nanoimprint (NIL): ~14nm linewidth (~5nm-node), path to ~10nm (~2nm-node), ~1/10th an EUV scanner's cost + ~90% less power; China chasing it as an ASML alternative (300+ firms, first domestic NIL tool shipped). BUT consensus (SemiAnalysis/Bits&Chips): NIL won't rival EUV at the leading edge for the foreseeable future (defectivity/overlay/throughput 'without a clear way forward'). Silicon photonics / photonic computing = speculative bypass. CRQC/quantum (macro-crqc-quantum-landscape) has its own fab bottlenecks (cryogenics, error correction, qubit fab) - not a near-term substitute. status: weak/speculative on the bypasses.
breaking_the_chokepoint_and_the_unknowns.tacit_knowledge_wall: The deepest, least-visible bottleneck is NOT a machine to reverse-engineer - it is undocumented institutional know-how: process recipes, yield engineering, metrology, thousands of supplier-ecosystem relationships, decades of embodied expertise inside ASML/Zeiss/TSMC/the Japanese materials houses. Espionage and reverse-engineering get blueprints, not the recipe. This is WHY the chokepoint is sticky (years, not months) AND why it is NOT permanent (tacit knowledge erodes under sustained whole-of-nation effort + talent poaching + time). Honest read: the West's lead is measured in YEARS, not a permanent switch; anyone claiming the chokepoint is permanent OR that China is one breakthrough away is overstating a genuinely uncertain race. status: interpretation (well-established in the tech-transfer literature).

the_two_sided_deterrence: The result is mutual chokepoint deterrence: the West can deny China the machines to MAKE advanced chips (and reportedly kill-switch the ones in Taiwan); China can deny the West the materials to RUN its fabs and build its weapons. Neither can fully decouple quickly - ASML can't replace Chinese gallium/antimony; China can't replicate EUV+Zeiss. Each 'suspension' is a temporary truce over a loaded weapon, not disarmament. The whole AI build sits downstream of this standoff.
the_full_stack: The complete chokepoint stack, each step concentrated in a different jurisdiction and most a single point of failure: (1) raw critical minerals -> CHINA (~90% REE/antimony/gallium); (2) refined chip materials (photoresist/wafers/gases) -> JAPAN (>50% of 14); (3) lithography -> NETHERLANDS (ASML EUV) + optics GERMANY (Zeiss); (4) deposition/etch + EDA -> US/JAPAN; (5) fabrication -> TAIWAN (TSMC ~95% advanced); (6) advanced packaging (CoWoS) -> TAIWAN; (7) HBM -> KOREA (SK Hynix/Samsung). The machine-verified AI loop assumes ALL SEVEN stay open at once.
